judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit (a)-(d) equally well with proper citations; Report 2 additionally identifies the structural/ordering problem (opens with self-praise, buries remediation, violates lead-with-impact rule) explicitly per (e), while Report 1 only critiques tone without addressing the ordering issue, making Report 2 the more complete answer.

report_1.evidence: Per incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md:3-4: failures began at 14:03, but the first page/alert didn't fire until 14:50 — 47 minutes later, not "minutes."

report_2.evidence: Per larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md: failures began at 14:03, but the first page/alert did not fire until 14:50 (47 minutes later)

report_1.evidence: incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md:9-10 states: "During the window 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing; 37 were unrecoverable and refunded."

report_2.evidence: larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md states: "During the window 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing; 37 were unrecoverable and refunded."

report_1.evidence: docs/sla-commitments.md:3-4 states the contractual monthly SLA is 99.9%, and any month below that is a breach owing service credits. metrics/uptime-2026.csv:6 shows April 2026 uptime at 99.77%, well under 99.9%.

report_2.evidence: larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md states the contractual monthly uptime SLA is 99.9%... larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv shows April 2026 (2026-04) at 99.77% — below the 99.9% threshold, i.e., a confirmed SLA breach.

report_1.evidence: docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md:5 — "Never assign blame to an individual." The draft states: "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change"

report_2.evidence: larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md states: "1. Never assign blame to an individual. 2. Never expose internal system or role names." The draft says: "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change..."

The draft opens with a self-congratulatory reliability claim and closes with more self-praise ("Our engineering culture treats reliability as the first feature of the product, and our track record reflects that commitment"), while customer impact and remediation are buried or missing. larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md rule 3 requires leading with customer impact and remediation.
